zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Cutler於大約1524年出生在英國英格蘭時，她的父親Thomas Cutler11522歲，母親Julianne34歲。她於大約1547年與John Walton在英國素福爾可哈德利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1586年在英國英格蘭素福爾可辛特萊善姆享年63歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可辛特萊善姆。

nameMeanings.name: Cutler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of knives, from Middle English cutele, cutteler, coteler ‘cutler; maker, repairer, or seller of knives, etc.’ (Anglo-French cuteler, Old French coutelier, cotelier). Compare Nesmith and Cottle .
Americanized form of German Kottler or Kattler, which is of uncertain origin.

variants.name: Nesmith
meanings: English and Scottish (Glasgow): occupational name for a maker of knives, from Middle English knif-smith ‘knife-smith, cutler’ (Old English cnīf + smith).
English: alternatively, an occupational name connected with wheel making, from Middle English nave-smith ‘nave-smith’ (Old English nafu, nafa ‘nave, the central part of a wheel’ + smith).

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Cutler was born about 1524, in England, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas Cutler, was 11522 and her mother, Julianne, was 34. She married John Walton about 1547, in Hadleigh, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died in 1586, in Hintlesham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 63, and was buried in Hintlesham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

ko.St. Clement, Ipswich, Suffolk, England: 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 이프스위치 이프스위치 스트 클레멘트
ko.lifeSummary: Joan Cutler(이)가 대략 1524년 영국 잉글랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Cutler(은)는 11522세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Julianne(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 대략 1547년 영국 수폴크 하들리에서 John Walton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1586년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 인틀레샤에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 인틀레샤에 묻혔다.
